title: Assumption ledger entry
description: One assumption recorded under .praxis/assumptions/log.jsonl by the 'never assume, always validate' rule. Recorded when an agent proceeds on a low-stakes, reversible choice without certainty; a material ambiguity is a stop condition, not an assumption. Resolved by a sweep into a durable decision and, when it generalises, a proposed promotion to a rule/guardrail/eval/gate.

created.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the assumption was recorded.

updated.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last status change.

source.description: The step, agent, or command that made the assumption, e.g. /developer.

status.description: Lifecycle: open -> confirmed | corrected | withdrawn. Drawn from a closed set, never invented.

confidence.description: How sure the agent was. High confidence is not an assumption — record a decision instead.

impact.description: What the assumption affects (artifact, behavior, architecture).

alternatives.description: Other answers the choice could have taken; rendered as options B, C, ... in the sweep.

promote.description: On resolution, the kind of durable control this answer is PROPOSED to become. Proposed via the memory ledger as pending — never applied silently.

decision_ref.description: Id of the memory-ledger entry that records the user's adjudication.

resolution.description: The correct answer supplied by the user when the assumption was corrected.
